target.name: Safe Superintelligence

methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $5.6B.

data.narrative:
Safe Superintelligence is a Series B AI & Software company founded by Ilya Sutskever, Daniel Gross, and Daniel Levy, valued at $32.0B post-money. The peer universe consists of 1 public comparable (Palantir) and 8 private AI software companies, with the most relevant stage-matched peers being Physical Intelligence ($5.6B), World Labs ($5.0B), Nominal ($1.0B), and Photoroom ($500M).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $32.0B last post-money valuation and applying a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier typical of venture progression, implies a forward valuation of $60.8B. Peer-median analysis across the four same-sector, same-stage comparables yielded a median valuation of $3.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.6B. The substantial disparity between these methods reflects the company's outsized current valuation relative to its peer cohort.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $2.5B to $69.9B, with a median of $31.9B. The current $32.0B valuation aligns closely with the triangulated median, anchored primarily by stage-progression methodology. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of peer multiples in a rapidly evolving frontier AI market and the applicability of historical Series B step-up multiples to companies at this valuation scale and stage.
